jokes.text: Last time I was this excited was this morning. I saw a cat wearing the same sweater as its person.
jokes.setup: Building up excitement for the America's Kidz finale
jokes.punchline: Deflating it with mundane observation about matching cat/owner outfits
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with good misdirection - sets up TV finale excitement then deflates with perfectly mundane pet observation

jokes.text: So, Jack, how's your wife? Still kidnapped?
jokes.setup: Polite social greeting about spouse
jokes.punchline: Casually asking about ongoing kidnapping like it's normal news
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - treats kidnapping with casual social politeness. Excellent character voice for precocious teen villain

jokes.text: Not that it matters, even if Brock doesn't win, he is going to get a record contract. So, it definitely doesn't matter. This isn't the last we will be seeing of Brock.
jokes.setup: Claiming the competition outcome doesn't matter
jokes.punchline: Obsessively repeating how much it doesn't matter, proving it absolutely does
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Kaylie's transparent attempt to seem casual while clearly invested

jokes.text: Kidz is a hit the whole family can watch together, not your usual sitcom crap full of gratuitous vulgarity and pratfalls. My penis!
jokes.setup: Jack praising family-friendly content without vulgarity
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by someone shouting about their penis
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Jack's speech about clean content immediately undercut by exactly what he's criticizing

jokes.text: Now, of course, none of these little condom accidents could actually make me cry
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her plan to cry during children's performance
jokes.punchline: Referring to child contestants as 'condom accidents'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jenna's narcissistic cruelty - dark humor that's character-specific and shocking

jokes.text: It's an old acting trick I learned from Glenn Beck's prostitute.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining where she learned a fake crying technique
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Glenn Beck's prostitute as her acting coach
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with perfect specificity - the randomness of Glenn Beck + prostitute + acting coach is brilliantly weird

jokes.text: That was my angriest hug, 'cause that's how I feel right now.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving Jack a hug
jokes.punchline: Explaining it was specifically an 'angry hug' to match his emotions
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - the concept of categorizing hugs by emotion level is perfectly in character

jokes.text: The Federal Clown Commission. I hope that comes off as respectful. My father was a clown, and I know how hard those men work.
jokes.setup: Tracy mishearing FCC as Federal Clown Commission
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with fake backstory about his clown father
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy malapropism with commitment to the bit - treating clown work as serious profession

jokes.text: People have forgotten about that thing, but any white male can arrest any other person.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning detaining people under Patriot Act
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong interpretation that it gives white men arrest powers
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire disguised as character ignorance - Jack's privilege showing through absurd legal interpretation

jokes.text: Because of last night's cluster-whoops
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing the TV disaster
jokes.punchline: Using 'cluster-whoops' instead of the obvious profanity
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific euphemism - Kenneth's wholesome nature creating innocent version of profanity

jokes.text: I feel like Oscar the grouch today, and not just 'cause I woke up in a garbage can this morning, startling someone named Gordon.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing himself to Oscar the Grouch emotionally
jokes.punchline: Revealing he literally woke up in a garbage can with a stranger
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts with metaphor then reveals literal situation. Perfect Tracy absurdism

jokes.text: You're the one who solved that? Thank you so much.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning her college apartheid protest
jokes.punchline: Tracy crediting her personally with ending apartheid
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - earnestly thanking Liz for single-handedly solving major historical injustice

jokes.text: 1997 Heather Locklear, what are you doing here?
jokes.setup: Tracy seeing Jenna approaching
jokes.punchline: Identifying her as a very specific vintage of celebrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - not just comparing to Heather Locklear but specifying the exact year vintage

jokes.text: God. Don't adopt. That child is better off in Somalia. It could be a pirate, or a warlord's concubine.
jokes.setup: Jenna considering adoption for publicity
jokes.punchline: Liz suggesting Somalia offers better career prospects than being Jenna's child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with escalating absurd career options - treats piracy/concubinage as preferable to Jenna's parenting

jokes.text: Cashing your dead aunt's social security checks? No, I'm still doing that.
jokes.setup: Liz guessing Jenna's Chicago money-making scheme
jokes.punchline: Jenna casually confirming she's still committing that fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna casual criminality - treating ongoing fraud as minor side hobby

jokes.text: the calumet city egg donation center and house of blues
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining where she donated eggs
jokes.punchline: Combining medical facility with music venue in one business
jokes.explanation: Absurdist business combination - mixing reproductive services with entertainment venue

jokes.text: Hang on, are you saying you have a kid? No, I don't. I have six kids.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jenna has one child
jokes.punchline: Jenna correcting that she has six instead
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - denies having 'a kid' then reveals much worse situation

jokes.text: Jerome, share the bronzer, you slut.
jokes.setup: Meeting Jenna's biological children
jokes.punchline: Child using adult insults over makeup sharing
jokes.explanation: Shows the children have inherited Jenna's shallow, dramatic tendencies through makeup priorities

jokes.text: Little league tryouts are coming soon. How would you like to wind up on a team with... None of your friends?
jokes.setup: Jack threatening a child with intimidating consequences
jokes.punchline: The 'threat' is mild social inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack intimidation style - using executive power for petty childhood social dynamics

jokes.text: What are you doing to Molly? She already had to live through world war ii.
jokes.setup: Child concerned about interrogation tactics
jokes.punchline: Claiming their doll survived WWII
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist child logic - treating doll as historical trauma survivor

jokes.text: Like a human silly band!
jokes.setup: Child describing why someone is cool
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to a rubber bracelet toy
jokes.explanation: Kid logic humor - using trendy toy as highest form of coolness compliment

jokes.text: I'm tall enough now that there isn't a roller coaster in the world I can't go on.
jokes.setup: Kaylie boasting about her newfound power
jokes.punchline: The power is just meeting height requirements for rides
jokes.explanation: Good teen perspective - treating amusement park access as ultimate freedom

jokes.text: You guys drink beer. Or are you babies?
jokes.setup: Kaylie trying to pressure adults
jokes.punchline: Using playground-level peer pressure tactics on grown-ups
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - teen using childish tactics while trying to seem adult

jokes.text: Left, left, right, right, left... Judy.
jokes.setup: Jenna identifying everyone's good camera angles
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Judy as if she doesn't have a good side
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna cruelty - treating person as purely aesthetic object then rejecting them

jokes.text: If you want to get to know me, read my 2006 interview in AmTrak magazine.
jokes.setup: Daughter wanting to connect personally with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Jenna directing her to obscure train magazine interview
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna response - avoiding human connection by referencing bizarrely specific publicity

jokes.text: No, 'Kenneth' is short for andromakennethamblesorton.
jokes.setup: Kenneth correcting that 'Kenneth' is his nickname
jokes.punchline: Revealing impossibly long, bizarre full name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kenneth weirdness - creating elaborate backstory for simple name correction

jokes.text: I'm surprised they allow you to say 'doctor.' It's so close to 'gynecologist' which is disgusting.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining new broadcasting restrictions
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic connecting 'doctor' to gynecologist
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth prudishness - creating nonsensical word association censorship

jokes.text: Kenneth is being a real... dingbat, which is now the harshest word we are allowed to say.
jokes.setup: Liz wanting to insult Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Being forced to use the mildest possible insult due to new standards
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about censorship restrictions affecting character expression

jokes.text: That's why I hired a Cato to attack me at random. Like inspector clouseau.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he maintains his edge
jokes.punchline: Hiring someone to randomly attack him like in Pink Panther
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack executive paranoia - taking movie gag seriously as business strategy

jokes.text: Excellent, Cato, excellent!
jokes.setup: Jack's earlier mention of hiring Cato
jokes.punchline: Cato actually attacking him as promised
jokes.explanation: Solid callback payoff - delivers on setup promise with physical comedy

jokes.text: Autocorrect. I was trying to say 'pen organizer.'
jokes.setup: Jack's phone showing embarrassing autocorrect
jokes.punchline: Claiming he was trying to type something completely unrelated
jokes.explanation: Good autocorrect humor with absurd original word claim

jokes.text: God! You're a winter!
jokes.setup: Child insulting someone
jokes.punchline: Using seasonal fashion advice as devastating insult
jokes.explanation: Funny child-specific cruelty using fashion terminology as serious insult

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Judy. Bye, Judy. Could've just gotten another chair.
jokes.setup: Elaborate elimination process to exclude Judy
jokes.punchline: Judy pointing out the obvious simple solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to manufactured drama - simple logic defeating elaborate cruelty

jokes.text: I'm very good at voices, Kaylie. I'm very good.
jokes.setup: Jack doing fake grandfather voice
jokes.punchline: Proudly defending his clearly terrible impression
jokes.explanation: Good Jack ego moment - taking pride in mediocre skill during manipulation

jokes.text: This has nothing to do with you!
jokes.setup: Jack assuming elaborate conspiracy against him
jokes.punchline: Kaylie revealing it's just teenage drama unrelated to Jack
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection deflating Jack's paranoid assumptions with mundane reality

jokes.text: Isn't that just a sweat shop? I know!
jokes.setup: Kaylie's mom's charity where poor children make shoes
jokes.punchline: Both casually acknowledging it's obviously exploitative
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as casual observation about privilege blindness

jokes.text: I once took a log with googly eyes to a father-son picnic.
jokes.setup: Jack relating to absent parents
jokes.punchline: Revealing his pathetic childhood substitute father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack backstory - perfect specificity and pathos in absurd childhood memory

jokes.text: No, there's only one of me. What? What are you asking?
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Kenneth has 'a second'
jokes.punchline: Kenneth interpreting it as asking if there's another Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth literal interpretation creating confusion from common phrase

jokes.text: You can't say 'using' on TV. It implies drug use.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining new word restrictions
jokes.punchline: Banning common word for ridiculous association
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of censorship absurdity - banning basic vocabulary for tenuous connections

jokes.text: I am responding, I am matching your tone, and making my own point.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to argue with Liz
jokes.punchline: Mechanically describing what arguing is instead of actually arguing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - Kenneth explaining argument structure while failing to argue

jokes.text: I stole that speech from an episode JAG, but it applies here.
jokes.setup: Kenneth giving a commanding speech
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that he plagiarized it from TV
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth moment that deflates his authority while showing his TV obsession. JAG reference is perfect.

jokes.text: Tomorrow is my colonoscopy. Today was my meeting with Colin o'Scopy.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his calendar confusion
jokes.punchline: Treating 'colonoscopy' as a person named Colin O'Scopy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy wordplay - turning medical procedure into Irish person's name

jokes.text: Well, I was voted head of the PTA, so no.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack was a difficult teenager
jokes.punchline: Jack claiming he was head of PTA as a teenager
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack backstory - absurd teen overachievement that's somehow believable for his character

jokes.text: then on to that last bastion of the incompetent rich, USC.
jokes.setup: Jack describing downward trajectory for Kaylie
jokes.punchline: Dismissing USC as safety school for rich failures
jokes.explanation: Sharp class commentary disguised as Jack's casual elitism

jokes.text: She's vicious and vulnerable.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to categorize teenage girl
jokes.punchline: Liz pointing out they're both simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good insight into teenage psychology with economical delivery

jokes.text: but having glimpsed yet another tile in the rich mosaic that is your menstrual history.
jokes.setup: Jack leaving Liz's office confused
jokes.punchline: Pretentiously describing her TMI oversharing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack eloquence applied to crude topic - perfect character voice mixing high and low

jokes.text: and you guys are suckling on prosthetic dog teats that I'm wearing.
jokes.setup: Jenna pitching reality show concept
jokes.punchline: Involving her children nursing from fake dog parts
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna inappropriateness - sexualizing maternal relationship for TV concept

jokes.text: That's Hugh Hefner, Jessica. Yeah, we have no idea who that is, so you're kind of just proving our point.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining boat captain reference
jokes.punchline: Daughter not knowing cultural reference, making Jenna seem older
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Jenna's reference backfiring to prove her obsolescence

jokes.text: and sit down and pee in silence, like dudes do.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to comfort Kenneth in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Claiming men normally sit to pee silently
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - incorrect masculine behavior observation

jokes.text: without Liz Lemon yelling at me, and I just wish I had never been born!
jokes.setup: Kenneth complaining about job stress
jokes.punchline: Escalating to wanting to never exist over workplace conflict
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Kenneth's dramatic overreaction to minor workplace issues

jokes.text: and boss around those Jewish executives that were trained from birth to argue?
jokes.setup: Kenneth expressing career ambitions
jokes.punchline: Revealing casual antisemitic stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate prejudice humor but relies on offensive stereotype for shock value

jokes.text: You should just smack that Liz in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking for advice
jokes.punchline: Terrible advice from his mirror self
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Kenneth dual identity gag nicely

jokes.text: You should just smack that Liz in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking stranger for advice
jokes.punchline: Getting terrible violent advice from random bathroom encounter
jokes.explanation: Good setup for recurring gag - introducing bad influence character

jokes.text: I'm Kenneth, by the way. Me too. I'm Kenneth. Kenneth... uh... Toilethole.
jokes.setup: Two Kenneths meeting in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Second Kenneth having bathroom-appropriate surname
jokes.explanation: Good coincidence humor with location-appropriate name

jokes.text: I'm her Nemesis.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking Jack's relationship to student
jokes.punchline: Jack formally identifying as her nemesis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack formality applied to absurd relationship - treating teen rivalry as official position

jokes.text: A Parcell man has never been called 'mister' outside of an execution chamber.
jokes.setup: Liz calling Kenneth 'mister'
jokes.punchline: Kenneth revealing family history with death penalty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth backstory - dark family history revealed through etiquette correction

jokes.text: They threw me away like I was some kind of Judy.
jokes.setup: Jenna feeling rejected by her children
jokes.punchline: Using Judy's name as generic term for unwanted person
jokes.explanation: Good callback turning character name into generic insult category

jokes.text: My brain is working overtime. I finally understand the ending of The sixth sense. Those names are the people who worked on the movie.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming mental clarity from fasting
jokes.punchline: Confusing movie credits with plot revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - treating film credits as mysterious plot element

jokes.text: Son of a dingbat!
jokes.setup: Jack realizing he's been outmaneuvered
jokes.punchline: Using the only allowed curse word
jokes.explanation: Good callback to censorship rules affecting Jack's moment of realization

jokes.text: You know why I love Dominican women? 'Cause they love to those big 'Cause they eat all our aloud.
jokes.setup: Tracy doing stand-up act on live TV
jokes.punchline: Kenneth bleeping him so aggressively it makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - censorship so extreme it destroys meaning entirely

jokes.text: I'm promoting you, you little -face.
jokes.setup: Standards head praising Kenneth's bleeping
jokes.punchline: Using profanity while discussing censorship success
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - censorship boss using bleeped profanity

jokes.text: I just had fruit roll-ups for dinner... At a strip club.
jokes.setup: Kaylie boasting about lack of supervision
jokes.punchline: Combining childish snack with adult venue
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between innocent food and inappropriate location

jokes.text: Her name is Jack... ie. Jackie... officecouch.
jokes.setup: Kaylie claiming she planted evidence against someone
jokes.punchline: Making up obviously fake name on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good teen lying behavior - obviously improvised fake name

jokes.text: You know, I'm starting to suspect that I have bad luck.
jokes.setup: Liz missing Kenneth Toilethole repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about her constant misfortune
jokes.explanation: Good Liz understatement - treating constant disasters as mere suspicion of bad luck

jokes.text: You know, I'm starting to suspect that I have bad luck.
jokes.setup: Kenneth repeatedly missing the other Kenneth
jokes.punchline: His massive understatement about his consistently bad timing
jokes.explanation: Good understatement that perfectly captures Kenneth's mild reaction to obvious patterns.
